NCT02277743.name: SOLO-1
NCT02277743.group: Moderate-severe AD, dupilumab 300 mg SC Q2W vs placebo (16 wk monotherapy)
NCT02277743.snippet: Source: Simpson EL et al. N Engl J Med 2016;375:2335-2348 (SOLO-1).

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.text: 671 adults with moderate-to-severe atopic dermatitis (IGA >=3, EASI >=16, BSA >=10%) inadequately controlled with topicals randomized 1:1:1 double-blind to dupilumab 300 mg SC weekly (n=224), 300 mg SC every 2 weeks (n=224), or placebo (n=224) for 16 weeks monotherapy.

evidence.label: Co-Primary - IGA 0/1 at Week 16
evidence.text: Proportion achieving IGA 0/1 (clear/almost clear) at week 16: dupilumab Q2W 85/224 (38%) vs placebo 23/224 (10%); odds ratio 5.68 (95% CI 3.45 to 9.37; P<0.001). Co-primary endpoint met.

evidence.label: Secondary - EASI-75 and Itch NRS
evidence.text: EASI-75 at week 16: dupilumab Q2W 115/224 (51%) vs placebo 33/224 (15%); OR 5.58 (95% CI 3.58 to 8.72). Peak pruritus NRS >=4-point improvement: 41% vs 12%.

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.text: D1 LOW - central IVR randomization. D2 LOW - double-blind matched placebo SC injections. D3 LOW - 88% completion. D4 LOW - blinded central photograph scoring for IGA. D5 LOW - SAP pre-specified and locked.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 18 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~1. Male or female, 18 years or older~2. Chronic AD (according to American Academy of Dermatology Consensus Criteria Eichenfield 2014) that has been present for at least 3 years before the screening visit;~3. Eczema Area and Severity Index (EASI) Score ≥16 at the screening and baseline visits;~4. Investigator's Global Assessment (IGA) Score ≥3 (on the 0 to 4 IGA scale, in which 3 is moderate and 4 is severe) at the screening and baseline visits;~5. ≥10% body surface area (BSA...

NCT02277769.name: SOLO-2
NCT02277769.group: Moderate-severe AD, dupilumab 300 mg SC Q2W vs placebo (16 wk monotherapy)
NCT02277769.snippet: Source: Simpson EL et al. N Engl J Med 2016;375:2335-2348 (SOLO-2).

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.text: 708 adults with moderate-to-severe AD randomized 1:1:1 double-blind to dupilumab 300 mg SC weekly (n=239), 300 mg SC every 2 weeks (n=233), or placebo (n=236) for 16 weeks monotherapy. SOLO-2 was the replicate pivotal trial to SOLO-1.

evidence.label: Co-Primary - IGA 0/1 at Week 16
evidence.text: Proportion achieving IGA 0/1 at week 16: dupilumab Q2W 84/233 (36%) vs placebo 20/236 (8%); odds ratio 6.40 (95% CI 3.80 to 10.78; P<0.001). Co-primary endpoint met, replicating SOLO-1.

evidence.label: Secondary - EASI-75 and Patient-Reported Outcomes
evidence.text: EASI-75 at week 16: dupilumab Q2W 103/233 (44%) vs placebo 27/236 (12%); OR 5.50 (95% CI 3.44 to 8.79). Peak pruritus NRS >=4-point improvement, DLQI, HADS all significantly improved.

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.text: D1 LOW - central randomization stratified by region and baseline severity. D2 LOW - double-blind matched placebo. D3 LOW - 89% completion. D4 LOW - blinded central photograph-based IGA scoring. D5 LOW - SAP locked.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 18 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~1. Chronic AD that has been present for at least 3 years before the screening visit;~2. ≥10% body surface area (BSA) of AD involvement at the screening and baseline visits;~3. Documented recent history (within 6 months before the screening visit) of inadequate response to treatment with topical medications or for whom topical treatments are otherwise medically inadvisable (eg, because of important side effects or safety risks).~Exclusion Criteria:~1. Participation in a prior...

NCT02260986.name: CHRONOS
NCT02260986.group: Moderate-severe AD, dupilumab 300 mg Q2W + TCS vs placebo + TCS (52 wk)
NCT02260986.snippet: Source: Blauvelt A et al. Lancet 2017;389:2287-2303 (CHRONOS).

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.text: 740 adults with moderate-to-severe AD inadequately controlled by topicals randomized 3:1:3 double-blind to dupilumab 300 mg SC weekly + TCS (n=319), 300 mg SC Q2W + TCS (n=106), or placebo + TCS (n=315) for 52 weeks.

evidence.label: Co-Primary - IGA 0/1 at Week 16 (+ TCS)
evidence.text: Proportion achieving IGA 0/1 at week 16: dupilumab Q2W + TCS 125/319 (39%) vs placebo + TCS 39/315 (12%); odds ratio 4.64 (95% CI 3.11 to 6.92; P<0.0001). Co-primary endpoint met.

evidence.label: Secondary - Durable Response at Week 52
evidence.text: IGA 0/1 sustained through week 52: dupilumab Q2W + TCS 36% vs placebo + TCS 13%. EASI-75 at wk16 dupilumab 217/319 (68%) vs placebo 74/315 (23%); OR 4.70 (95% CI 3.37-6.56). Safety: injection-site reactions and conjunctivitis higher with dupilumab.

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.text: D1 LOW - central stratified randomization. D2 LOW - double-blind matched placebo schedule with identical TCS regimen. D3 LOW - 81% completion at 52 wk. D4 LOW - blinded central IGA adjudication. D5 LOW - SAP locked.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 18 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Key Inclusion Criteria:~1. Chronic AD that had been present for at least 3 years before the screening visit;~2. Documented recent history (within 6 months before the screening visit) of inadequate response to a sufficient course of out-patient treatment with topical AD medication(s).~Key Exclusion Criteria:~1. Participation in a prior Dupilumab clinical trial;~2. Important side effects of topical medication (e.g. intolerance to treatment, hypersensitivity reactions, significant skin atrophy, sy...

title: RapidMeta Dermatology | Dupilumab in Moderate-to-Severe Atopic Dermatitis v1.0
